harvest_method: Playwright headless browser against live Amazon.com product-detail pages (https://www.amazon.com/dp/[ASIN]) plus the Kindle 'vagus nerve' review-rank search page. Real Amazon Best Sellers Rank, current price, star rating, and Amazon review count read directly from each rendered product page. ZERO interpolated/synthetic values. Fields that genuinely do not exist on the live page (e.g. unranked brand-new releases with no BSR, or Kindle prices not exposed in a clean selector) are flagged explicitly rather than invented.
ku_penetration_pct: Not separately quantified this run; Kindle search results display 'Read with Kindle Unlimited / Available instantly' rather than numeric prices, indicating heavy KU presence in the Kindle tier.

data_confidence.titles_asins_authors: real - verified from live Amazon.com product pages
data_confidence.publish_dates: real where present - from Amazon/Goodreads/publisher (unchanged from prior harvest)
data_confidence.review_counts: real - Amazon on-page review counts captured via Playwright (supersede prior Goodreads figures)
data_confidence.ratings: real - Amazon on-page star ratings captured via Playwright
data_confidence.prices: real - current live prices read from product pages on 2026-06-19; a few Kindle-only edition prices flagged where not cleanly exposed
data_confidence.bsr: real - live Amazon Best Sellers Rank captured via Playwright; brand-new unranked releases flagged 'no BSR assigned (new release)'

kindle_search_review_rank_top_results.note: Kindle Store search sorted by review-rank. Prices are NOT displayed on Kindle search cards (shown as Read with Kindle Unlimited / Available instantly), so price is null here; titles, ASINs, ratings, and Amazon review counts are real and live. NOTE: most of these ASINs are NEW competitors NOT in the 20-ASIN harvest above -- this is a second, higher-review competitor tier the original harvest missed.

key_findings: Live BSR successfully captured for 17 of 20 ASINs via Playwright. The 3 unranked are brand-new 2025 releases with 0 reviews (Gillette, Sterling-no-reviews-but-ranked is actually ranked; the true unranked are Gillette B0GHZQQGZF, Daniele ACG B0GP6S76DK, and Whitman has a deep rank). Correction: unranked-with-0-reviews = Gillette and Daniele ACG; Sterling and Whitman ARE ranked deep (#3.9M, #5.8M).

BSR reality check: the BEST-selling indie competitor is Adele Payne 'The Vagus Nerve Protocol' at BSR #118,524 with 352 reviews / 4.7 stars -- the strongest indie by both rank and social proof. Second is Casey Bennett's workbook at #40,892 (paperback) with 199 reviews / 4.7. These two are the real indie leaders.

Most indie titles sit at DEEP BSR (#1M - #5.8M), confirming the niche has many low-velocity entrants. Strong reviews do not guarantee strong rank: Michael Stone has 61 reviews but BSR #5.1M; getting reviews early via giveaways inflates social proof faster than it lifts rank.

The trad-pub anchors dominate true velocity: Rosenberg #14,078 (2,632 reviews), Tracey #19,568 (262 reviews), Ferguson #33,274 (415 reviews). These are the actual demand drivers; indies feed off the long tail.

Neuroanatomy is the soft-underbelly category: multiple indies rank top-20 there (Payne #4, Walters #14, Nicholson #19, Clement #103) despite weak overall BSR. This is the realistic category to chart in.
Indie Kindle pricing is a race to the bottom: $0.99 loss-leaders are everywhere (Walters, Stone, Clement). Paperback is where indie revenue actually sits, clustered $14.90-$15.99.

The Kindle review-rank search surfaced a SECOND competitor tier the 20-ASIN harvest missed entirely -- e.g. 'The Vagus Nerve Solution' (B0DHJDG1NB, 235 reviews), 'The Vagus Nerve Switch' (B0FY818Z7H, 230, 8-minute/digestion hook), 'Unlock The Power of Your Vagus Nerve' (B0F3Y3NTBF, 211, gut-health hook). These higher-review Kindle competitors should be added to the next harvest pass.

Gut-brain / digestion hook is being claimed by the higher-review Kindle tier (B0FY818Z7H 'digestion', B0DH9F7R4W 'brain-gut harmony', B0F3Y3NTBF 'gut health') -- the gut angle is LESS open than the prior blueprint assumed. Differentiation needs sharper than 'gut' alone.
limitations: Kindle search-result cards do not expose numeric prices (KU/instant badges only); per-ASIN Kindle prices for the search tier would require visiting each product page.
Three brand-new indie releases have no BSR/reviews yet (too new to rank) -- this is a real state, not a scrape failure.
A few Kindle-only older eBooks (Hoyd, Sterling, LifeQuest) did not expose a clean Kindle price selector; flagged rather than invented.
